title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Independent Server Trial
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure scoped Codex STDIO or HTTP servers, timeouts, tools, approvals, and authentication.
Configure Claude Code local, project, or user MCP scope and review shared server trust.
Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
